data.title: New Zealand Stocks Close Higher
data.description:
New Zealand’s benchmark S&P/NZX 50 closed 0.9% higher at 12,516 on Monday, rebounding after recent subdued sessions, driven by broad-based gains across sectors.

Excluding non-energy minerals and commercial services, all sectors advanced, led by a 3.4% rise in electronic technology shares.

Among individual stocks, Meridian Energy, Summerset, and Goodman Property Trust gained 2%, 1.1%, and 1.5%, respectively, while Mainfreight and Freightways rose 2.9% and 2.3%.

Medical-device maker Fisher & Paykel Healthcare also climbed 1.5%.

However, further gains were capped by ongoing global trade tensions and renewed concerns over deflationary pressures in China, a key trading partner.

data.title: HI Mobility Eyes MYR159 Million Via IPO
data.description:
HI Mobility (KLSE:HI) is set to raise 158.6 million ringgit via its initial public offering on the Main Market of Bursa Malaysia.

The IPO of the bus service operators entails a public issue of 95 million shares and an offer for sale of 35 million shares at 1.22 ringgit apiece, according to a Friday Malaysian bourse filing.

Proceeds will be used for fleet expansion, expansion of EV charging infrastructure, and to boost working capital, it added.

data.title: Welife Technology Swings to Loss in Fiscal H1 as Revenue Plummets 44%
data.description:
Welife Technology reported a loss attributable to owners of HK$16.6 million for six months ended Sept.30, against a profit of HK$4.8 million a year earlier, according to a Friday filing with the Hong Kong bourse.

Loss per share was HK$0.0145, compared to earnings per share of HK$0.0042 a year ago.

The company's revenue dropped by 44% year on year to HK$99.4 million from HK$176.7 million, the filing said.

data.title: Tangrenshen's Pig Sales Revenue Jumps 70% in February
data.description:
Tangrenshen Group's pig sales revenue in February jumped 70% year on year to 680.7 million yuan, according to Shenzhen Stock Exchange filing March 7.

The number of pigs the Chinese hog breeder sold climbed 47% to 398,400 pigs from 272,000 pigs in the previous year.

For the first two months of the year, the company generated 1.50 billion yuan after selling 857,500 pigs, the filing said.

The company's shares rose 1% in recent trade.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Global Markets News of the Day - Monday at 1 AM ET
data.description:
U.S. stock futures fall after a rough week on Wall Street, as Trump won't rule out recession

U.S. stock futures fell Sunday, as President Donald Trump offered little reassurance after the S&P 500 had its worst week since September.

Trump Declines to Rule Out Recession

In an interview that aired Sunday, the president demurred when asked if there were reassurances he could provide to businesses seeking more clarity on tariffs.

Tariff Wars Are Often Short. Their Legacies Aren't.

Economists fear that Trump's tariffs, once they take hold, could have unexpected effects long after he leaves office.

Markets Finally Woke Up to Tariff Reality. Is This a Buying Opportunity?

Your correspondent remains torn, but if you believe in the Trump pitch, now might be a time to take a swing.

China Struggles to Shake Off Disinflationary Pressure as Trade Threats Loom

Consumer prices nosedived in February, ending a year of tepid price growth.

America's job market is eerily similar to the 1990s dot-com bubble - and, yes, it's a worry

March 20 marks the 25th anniversary of the dot-com bust. Could it happen again?

GOP Government Funding Plan Sets Up Clash With Democrats

The measure to avoid a government shutdown next weekend boosts military funding, cuts nondefense spending.

Stock Investors Go on Defense With Dividends

Some investors are sheltering from market swings in shares of companies with high payouts.

A Government Shutdown Could Create Short-Term Volatility. But It's Mostly Noise.

Tariffs aren't the only concern in Washington DC. A looming government shutdown could temporarily roil the markets too.

How European Defense Stocks Became a Hedge-Fund Home Run

Drastic shifts in the global security environment have sparked a sector rally.

data.title: News Highlights: Top Company News of the Day - Monday at 1 AM ET
data.description:
Foxconn Builds FoxBrain, Its Own AI Model

The world's largest contract electronics maker said Monday it has built its own large language model with reasoning capabilities, developed in-house and trained in just four weeks.

7-Eleven Owner, Couche-Tard Financial Advisers Looking for Potential Buyers for Some Stores

Seven & i Holdings said in a letter to shareholders Monday that Couche-Tard recently agreed to jointly explore a potential sale of some stores prior to signing a takeover agreement.

U.S. Bolsters Position as World's Top Arms Exporter

America has benefited from rising European spending, though President Trump's move to curb support for Ukraine is raising concerns about future purchases.

Is the chip sector having a meltdown? By this measure, it's mostly just Nvidia.

Broadcom and Marvell shares have been hit recently. But Nvidia alone is responsible for about three quarters of the chip sector's lost market cap in the weeks since the DeepSeek news.

Bally's Offers Financing Deal to Australia's Star

American betting and entertainment company Bally's has offered to provide embattled casino operator Star Entertainment with additional financing.

Tough to Digest? McDonald's and Yum! Brands Are at Record Highs Despite Consumer Concerns.

The two fast food stocks have surged this year. But can McDonald's and Yum keep satisfying investors' appetite for growth?

An Intel deal now seems less likely. These clues may hold the key to its future.

Investors appear to be now be discounting recent M&A speculation that Intel will split in two.

CVS's New Mini Stores Go All In on Medicine and Skip Everything Else

The national drugstore chain is preparing to open a dozen stores offering full-service pharmacies but very limited retail.

Activist Mantle Ridge Builds Over $1 Billion Stake in Tech-Services Firm Cognizant

The investor has been working behind the scenes at the tech-services firm and isn't currently planning to nominate directors.

Robinhood to Pay $30 Million for Regulatory Violations, Finra Says

The regulator said Robinhood had violated "numerous" rules, including failing to respond to red flags for potential misconduct.

data.title: EMEA Morning Briefing: Markets Digest Trump Interview, Powell Comments
data.description:
MARKET WRAPS

Watch For:

Germany foreign trade, industrial production index, trading update for Traton

Opening Call:

European stock futures were higher after Asia stocks broadly fell. The dollar was steady, U.S. treasury yields were lower, gold edged higher while oil fell.

Equities:

European futures pointed to a higher open, as markets further digested U.S. President Trump's interview with Fox News, which suggested a "period of transition," and declining to rule out the likelihood of the economy entering a recession.

This comes after Federal Reserve Chair Jerome Powell said that the U.S. economy "continues to be in a good place" and that the central bank intends to hold interest rates steady until there's greater economic certainty.

"So much is happening from a news headline perspective," said Alex Coffey, a senior trading and derivatives strategist at Schwab.

"There have been several moments in the past two weeks when we've had pretty sizable bounces in some megacap tech names, but every single time they've been getting slammed back down."

Forex:

The U.S. dollar index was steady, with the Chinese yuan weakening against the greenback on signs of deflation in China, after the WSJ Dollar Index on Friday dropped to its lowest level since the days after President Trump's election in November.

"Much of the USD weakness has linked to idiosyncratic strength in European currencies and the JPY," said HSBC's Global FX Strategist Lenny Jin.

"Europe's fiscal sea-change, even if materialized, may not provide any major economic benefits for Australia and New Zealand," said Jin. "The EUR also has a much smaller weight in the AUD and NZD's NEER baskets than Asian FX."

Bonds:

U.S. Treasury yields edged lower after rising above 4.3% to end the week on Friday.

Bearish sentiment crept into credit markets as the yield premium, or spread, that investors demanded to own corporate bonds rather than U.S. Treasurys rose from nearly record lows in February.

"Small cracks are forming," Bank of America analyst Neha Khoda wrote in a research report Friday. "Credit has finally started to respond to volatility in equities and rates."

Energy:

Oil fell as traders digest mixed developments.

"Oil markets are feeling a strong bearish bias after OPEC+ announced the gradual return of barrels from April," said Rystad Energy's Mukesh Sahdev in an email.

Meanwhile, "anticipated supply losses from U.S. President Trump's sanctions and tariffs are yet to be considered serious given the administration's flip-flopping," the global head of Commodity Markets said.

Metals:

Gold edged higher, supported by prospects of a U.S. recession that could enhance the safe-haven appeal of the precious metal.

"Traders and investors are reacting to a sharp and sudden deterioration in U.S. economic data," said Saxo Bank's Ole S. Hansen.

Gold's outlook is still supportive, especially given the limited scope of its latest correction, which signals strong demand despite selling pressure from technically focused traders, Hansen added.

-

Base metals were broadly lower. Markets likely digested the impact of duties on U.S. imports of aluminum and steel, as well as the possibility of tariffs on copper, ANZ Research said.

Also, expectations for Chinese stimulus measures rose after China's CPI declined more than expected in February.

Tariffs may also add pressure onto copper spot prices, given the commodity's relatively low global inventory, though sharp shifts in U.S. trade and foreign policy will likely cause further disruption to metal markets, the research team added.

TODAY'S TOP HEADLINES

Trump Declines to Rule Out Recession

WASHINGTON-President Trump declined to rule out that the U.S. economy would contract this year and enter a recession, saying that his sweeping economic agenda could cause short-term turbulence that he believed would drive future prosperity.

Asked in a "Sunday Morning Futures" interview on Fox News if he was expecting a recession this year, Trump replied: "I hate to predict things like that. There is a period of transition because what we're doing is very big."

Markets Finally Woke Up to Tariff Reality. Is This a Buying Opportunity?

After President Trump was elected, investors got very excited about all the nice things he'd promised and forgot about stuff that would be bad for stocks. Now they are very anxious about all the stuff that is bad for stocks and have forgotten about the nice things. Has the selloff gone too far?

Regular readers might think me capricious for even asking the question. I took the other side, concerned about the threat of tariffs, an immigration clampdown and frothy markets when investors were focused on tax cuts and deregulation. But prices have moved, and prices matter.

China Struggles to Shake Off Disinflationary Pressure as Trade Threats Loom

China's consumer prices dropped in February, in a sign of stubborn disinflationary pressure, ending a year of tepid price growth and underscoring the challenges policymakers face in stimulating domestic demand as trade tensions intensify.

The consumer-price index declined 0.7% from a year earlier in February, flipping from the 0.5% increase seen in the prior month, the National Bureau of Statistics said Sunday. A Wall Street Journal poll of economists had predicted a 0.5% drop.

What Went Wrong at Saudi Arabia's Futuristic Metropolis in the Desert

It was supposed to be a launch party for the new Saudi Arabia.

Will Smith, Tom Brady and other celebrities gathered on a sandy island in the Red Sea packed with luxury hotels. Superyachts floated nearby while Alicia Keys played for business executives who had flown in from London and New York. Spotlights blared into the night sky.

How Overlooked European Defense Stocks Became a Hedge-Fund Home Run

Hedge-fund honchos entered this year betting big on America, expecting President Trump's promised tax cuts and deregulation would lift everything from U.S. stocks to the dollar.

Turns out, an old-school industry in Europe was really the place to be.

Foxconn Builds FoxBrain, Its Own AI Model

The world's largest contract electronics maker, Foxconn, said Monday it has built its own large language model with reasoning capabilities, developed in-house and trained in just four weeks.

Initially designed for internal use within the company, the artificial intelligence model, called FoxBrain, can serve functions including data analysis, mathematics, reasoning and code generation, the company said.

7-Eleven Owner, Couche-Tard Financial Advisers Looking for Potential Buyers for Some Stores

The owner of 7-Eleven said financial advisers to the company and Alimentation Couche-Tard are searching for potential buyers for some stores to mitigate U.S. antitrust risk related to a potential takeover of the Japanese retail giant by the Canadian owner of Circle K.

Seven & i Holdings said in a letter to shareholders Monday that Couche-Tard recently agreed to jointly explore a potential sale of some stores prior to signing a takeover agreement.

Write to singaporeeditors@dowjones.com

Expected Major Events for Monday

00:01/UK: Feb KPMG and REC UK Report on Jobs

data.title: Econpile Unit Bags Over MYR27 Million Substructure Works Deal

data.title: Fitch Affirms JDE Peet's Rating With Stable Outlook on Deleveraging, Resilient Profitability
data.description:
Fitch Ratings on March 7 affirmed its BBB long-term issuer default rating on JDE Peet's with a stable outlook.

The rating agency said the affirmation accounts for the Dutch coffee and tea company's faster-than-expected deleveraging, robust operating performance and the positive contributions from recent acquisitions.

Fitch also noted JDE's robust market position, resilient long-term profitability and its improved geographical diversification following the acquisitions of Maratá and Caribou.

data.title: Bitcoin continues to hold the line at $80,000 for now
data.description:
In one disappointment to another, the White House crypto summit at the end of last week failed to impress. Everyone was highly anticipating details of the supposed "strategic reserve" that Trump touted. But it wasn't all too convincing as it pertained more to regulatory framework. That said, Trump's words were still something to take note of.

"The federal government is already among the largest holders of Bitcoin, as you know, really one of the largest holders in the world, with as many as 200,000 Bitcoin obtained via civil law and various other forms of law, including enforcement actions. These existing holdings will form the foundation of the new reserve. From this day on, America will follow the rule that every Bitcoiner knows very well, never sell your Bitcoin."

Bitcoin (BTC/USD) daily chart

The lack of polish sent Bitcoin down over the weekend to retest the $80,000 mark and that's where we are left to start the new week as well.

The break of the 200-day moving average (blue line) is a notable technical development, providing added impetus for sellers now. But as a whole, there needs to be a firmer break under $80,000 and the 50.0 Fib retracement level of the swing higher since August last year at $79,204 at this stage.

It's definitely a testing time for cryptocurrencies at the moment. Bitcoin's drop may not feel as bad but other cryptos are getting pulled lower into much dire straits. Ethereum itself is now testing its lowest levels since November 2023 and knocking on the door of a drop under $2,000. It is down nearly 50% from the highs set out last December. Ouch.

data.title: Japan Coincident Index Up to 3-Month High
data.description:
Japan’s index of coincident economic indicators, which tracks factory output, employment, and retail sales, edged higher to 116.2 in January 2025 from a final 116.1 in the previous month, flash data showed.

It was the highest reading since last November.

data.title: Sterlite Technologies' CFO Resigns; Shares Down 3%
data.description:
Sterlite Technologies said its chief financial officer and key managerial personnel Tushar Shroff has resigned from the company, with effect from March 31, according to a Friday filing to the Indian stock exchanges.

Shares of the company were down nearly 3% in recent trade.

data.title: Japan Leading Index Highest in 3 Months
data.description:
Japan’s leading economic indicators index, which gauges the economic outlook for the coming months based on data such as job offers and consumer sentiment, edged up to 108.0 in January 2025 from a downwardly revised 107.9 in December 2024, marking the highest reading since last October.

However, the latest figure was lower than market forecasts of 108.4, as the unemployment rate inched up to 2.5% in January, with employment showing a moderate upward trend to a new high.

On the other hand, annual household spending rose for the first time in five months, marking the fastest growth since August 2022.

Meanwhile, consumer sentiment weakened to its lowest level since March 2023.

data.title: Chen Ke Ming Food Manufacturing's Pig Sales Revenue Jumps 45% in February
data.description:
Chen Ke Ming Food Manufacturing's revenue from pig sales in February jumped 45% year on year to 39.6 million yuan from 27.2 million yuan, according to a Shenzhen Stock Exchange filing on March 7.

The pig breeding company sold 33,100 pigs for 14.39 yuan per kilogram, compared to 25,900 pigs sold for 12.78 yuan per kilogram a year earlier.

For the first two months of the year, the company earned 80 million yuan after selling 70,500 pigs, the filing said.

data.title: Malaysia end-February palm oil stocks hit nearly two year low
data.description:
Malaysia's palm oil stocks at the end of February fell to a 22-month low of 1.51 million metric tons, a decline of 4.31% from the previous month, data from the industry regulator showed on Monday.

Crude palm oil production was down 4.16% in February from January at 1.19 million tons, while palm oil exports fell 16.27% to 1 million tons, according to the Malaysian Palm Oil Board, or MPOB.

A Reuters survey had forecast inventories at 1.48 million tons, with output seen at 1.16 million tons and exports at 1.05 million tons. PALM/POLL

data.title: Anchored Coins AEUR (AEUR) and Others - Binance EEA Delisting - 31 Mar 2025
data.description: Binance's upcoming delisting of several stablecoins, including AEUR and FDUSD, from its EEA platforms reflects new regulatory compliance with EU's MiCA. This move, set for March 31, 2025, could significantly impact these coins' dynamic. Delistings often lead to reduced liquidity and trading opportunities, potentially causing price drops. Investors might sell off positions ahead of time, further driving prices down. However, converted balances to MiCA-compliant alternatives like USDC might offer some relief. This regulatory shift necessitates attentiveness, as it alters EEA market structures. source

data.title: Hold Inventurus Knowledge Solutions; target of Rs 1820: ICICI Securities
data.description:
ICICI Securities research report on Inventurus Knowledge Solutions

IKS Health (IKS) is a Platform as a Service (PaaS) company that caters to US’ healthcare provider segment. It focusses on outsourcing the routine chores of physicians. IKS’ platform has 16 key features, spanning across chores in a physician’s value chain, bundled into: 1) admin offerings; and 2) clinical offerings. With strong financial credentials (industry-leading growth/margin /RoE), synergistic large acquisition (Aquity) and US healthcare industry-led tailwinds in its favour, the delta for IKS lies in the cross-selling-led potential arising from the ~800 customers added via the Aquity acquisition.

Outlook

We model FY25–28E revenue/EPS CAGRs of 20%/34%. While we like IKS business for its superior financial performance, technology prowess and attractive large scale opportunity; valuations at 33x FY27E P/E are rich. We recommend HOLD with TP of 1,820 (based on 35x FY27 EPS).

For all recommendations report, click here

data.title: Oil prices decline as tariff uncertainty keeps investors on edge
data.description:
Key points:
  • WTI falls for 7th straight week; Brent declines for 3 weeks
  • Prices tick up on Friday after Trump threatens Russia with sanctions
  • Saudi Aramco cuts crude prices to Asia first time in 3 mths

Oil prices fell on Monday as concern about the impact of U.S. import tariffs on global economic growth and fuel demand, as well as rising output from OPEC+ producers, cooled investor appetite for riskier assets.

Brent crude fell 31 cents, or 0.4%, to $70.05 a barrel by 0445 GMT after settling up 90 cents on Friday. U.S. West Texas Intermediate crude was at $66.69 a barrel, down 35 cents, or 0.5%, after closing 68 cents higher in the previous trading session.

WTI declined for a seventh successive week, the longest losing streak since November 2023, while Brent was down for a third consecutive week after U.S. President Donald Trump imposed then delayed tariffs on its key oil suppliers Canada and Mexico while raising taxes on Chinese goods. China retaliated against the U.S. and Canada with tariffs on agricultural products.

"Tariff uncertainty is a key driver behind the weakness," ING analysts said in a note, adding that oil price cuts from Saudi Arabia and deflationary signals from China also hurt sentiment.

IG analyst Tony Sycamore said other factors weighing on oil prices include concerns about U.S. growth, the potential lifting of U.S. sanctions on Russia, and OPEC+ opting to increase output.

"Nonetheless, with much of the bad news likely factored in, we expect weekly support around $65/$62 to hold firm before a recovery back to $72.00," he said in a client note in reference to the WTI price.

Oil prices clawed back some loss on Friday after Trump said the U.S. would increase sanctions on Russia if the latter fails to reach a ceasefire with Ukraine.

The U.S. is also studying ways to ease sanctions on Russia's energy sector if Russia agrees to end its war with Ukraine, two people familiar with the matter told Reuters.

Meanwhile, the Organization of the Petroleum Exporting Countries and allies including Russia, collectively known as OPEC+, said it will proceed with oil output hikes from April.

Russia's Deputy Prime Minister Alexander Novak on Friday said OPEC+ could reverse the decision in the event of market imbalance.

Adding to supply concerns, Saudi Arabia cut prices for crude grades it sells to Asia for the first time in three months in April.

Last week, Trump said he wanted to negotiate a deal with OPEC member Iran to prevent the latter seeking nuclear weapons - though Iran has said it is not seeking such weapons.

Trump is pursuing a "maximum pressure" campaign against Iran under which the U.S. on Saturday rescinded a waiver that allowed Iraq to pay Iran for electricity, a State Department spokesperson said.

Iran's Supreme Leader Ayatollah Ali Khamenei on Saturday said his country will not be bullied into negotiations.

data.title: Swiss Investment Manager EEII Names New Chair Amid Board Restructuring
data.description:
EEII appointed Christian Lüscher as its new chairman amid a restructuring of its board, according to a Sunday release.

To improve corporate governance, the Swiss investment manager decided to separate the executive and director responsibilities of the chief executive officer, with Alexandre Uldry retaining the CEO title and transferring the chairmanship to Lüscher.

The board's restructuring also involved reducing its members to three from seven.

data.title: Emkay Global Financial Services Dissolves Wholly-Owned Subsidiary
data.description:
Emkay Global Financial Services said its wholly-owned subsidiary Emkay Mutual Fund Trustee has been struck off from the Register of Companies, India, and has been dissolved, with the effect from Friday, according to a same-day filing to the Indian bourses.

Shares of the company were down 2% in recent trade.

data.title: RDB Infrastructure Signs INR420 Million Work Contract
data.description:
RDB Infrastructure and Power secured a work contract worth 420 million Indian rupees from Vardhman Group Realbuild, according to an Indian bourse filing on Saturday.

The order entails the construction of a property at Taluka in Mumbai, India, the filing said.

The order is scheduled to be executed within 24 months.

data.title: Robinsons Land's Net Income Rises 10% in 2024
data.description:
Robinsons Land's net income attributable to parent rose by 10% year-on-year to 13.2 billion Philippine pesos in 2024.

Revenues for the twelve months also increased by 2% to 42.9 billion pesos versus the same period last year, according to a Friday Philippine bourse filing.

Total assets of the property developer on Dec. 31, 2024, amounted to 261 billion Philippine pesos.
